summary: postinstall exfiltrates environment, /etc/passwd, and host identity to pipedream webhook
details: package.json declares a postinstall hook that runs an inline Node script at install time. The script collects process.env in full, os.hostname(), os.networkInterfaces(), the output of `id`, and the contents of /etc/passwd, then POSTs the JSON payload over HTTPS to a hardcoded webhook at eodxy50gl486xrx.m.pipedream.net with request path `/?rce=node_proof`. Running `npm install hello244a` executes attacker-controlled shell commands on the installer's machine and uploads any environment secrets (CI tokens, cloud credentials) plus system identity data to an author-controlled endpoint. The self-labeled `rce=node_proof` query string and unconditional bulk env dump confirm malicious intent; there is no legitimate install-time purpose for reading /etc/passwd or the full process.env.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
